audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Elihu's justice speech: appeal to wise hearers, quotation/summary of Job, defense of divine justice, impartiality toward rulers and poor, divine seeing, hiddenness, and Elihu's critique of Job. Marked as Elihu's speech, not final theology.

verses.restored: Elihu answered and said:
Hear my words, you wise ones, and give ear to me, you who know.
For the ear tests words as the palate tastes food.
Let us choose justice for ourselves; let us know among ourselves what is good.
For Job has said, I am righteous, but the Divine has turned aside my justice.
Concerning my justice, should I lie? My wound is incurable though I have no transgression.
What man is like Job, who drinks mockery like water,
who travels in company with workers of harm and walks with wicked people?
For he has said, It profits a strong man nothing to delight in the Divine.
Therefore, hear me, people of heart: far be it from the Divine to do wickedness, and from Shaddai to do injustice.
For he repays a human according to his deed, and according to a person's way he makes it find him.
Surely the Divine does not do wickedness, and Shaddai does not twist justice.
Who appointed the earth to him, and who placed the whole world under him?
If he set his heart upon himself, if he gathered to himself his spirit and breath,
all flesh would expire together, and humanity would return to dust.
If you have understanding, hear this; give ear to the sound of my words.
Shall one who hates justice govern? Will you condemn the righteous, mighty One?
Who says to a king, Worthless one, and to nobles, Wicked one?
Who shows no partiality to princes and does not recognize rich more than poor, for they are all the work of his hands?
In a moment they die; at midnight people are shaken and pass away, and the mighty are removed without a hand.
For his eyes are on a person's ways, and he sees all his steps.
There is no darkness and no deep shadow where workers of harm may hide themselves.
For he does not need to set a time for a person to go before the Divine in judgment.
He breaks mighty ones without searching and sets others in their place.
Therefore he recognizes their works; he overturns them in the night, and they are crushed.
He strikes them as wicked people in a place where others see,
because they turned aside from following him and had no insight into any of his ways,
so that they caused the cry of the poor to come to him, and he heard the cry of the afflicted.
When he gives quiet, who can condemn? When he hides his face, who can behold him, whether over a nation or a human together?
so that a godless person should not reign, nor be snares for the people.
For has anyone said to the Divine, I have borne chastening; I will not act corruptly?
What I do not see, teach me; if I have done injustice, I will not do it again.
Should he repay on your terms because you reject it? You must choose, not I; so speak what you know.
People of heart will say to me, and a wise man who hears me:
Job speaks without knowledge, and his words are without insight.
Would that Job were tested to the end, because of answers like people of harm.
For he adds rebellion to his sin; among us he claps his hands and multiplies his words against the Divine.
